What is the chance that an abnormal stool card test will actually turn out to be cancer? 53.8% of control patients responded correctly, compared with 71.1% of patients who received screening information (p for difference = .0007). Control n=133, RRR n=133, ARR n=136.

outcome: Accurate risk perceptions - studies without high risk of bias

DCS Informed Post: Group 1 Control (n=69) Mean 27.3 (SE 2.0); Group 2 Videobooklet (n=70) Mean 15.4 (SE 1.7). SE converted to SD: ctrl SD=2.0*sqrt(69)=16.61, exp SD=1.7*sqrt(70)=14.23

outcome: Decisional conflict - uninformed - without studies having high risk of bias
